My main objective this year is to enter Europe.
The proudest achievement was losing the fear.
The challenge now is to sustain the momentum.
My vision is a brand known across Europe.
For the first time, the ambition feels reachable.
It is funny. On our very first call, you used to apologize for your English every two minutes. You do not do that anymore.
I had forgotten that. A year ago a phone call in English was a wall. Last month I closed an investment in it. That is the achievement I am proudest of.
And it shows. You used to rehearse every line; now you improvise and even crack a joke. The momentum you have built is real. Do not lose it.
That is the plan. The real breakthrough this year was not a deal. It was the day English stopped being the wall and became the tool.
So pick one big objective for next year. Something that scares you a little. What is the vision?
Europe. My objective is to enter the market; my aspiration is a brand Paris knows. To get there I have to sustain this momentum and grow the team. For the first time, the ambition feels reachable.
I used to dread English calls; closing a deal in English is my proudest achievement.
We have real momentum, so my objective is to enter Europe this year.
I used to freeze on English calls; now I host the meeting.

Hi Milton, it's Karen. I've been thinking about everything we've built this past year, and I just wanted to say it out loud: you've come a long way. I still remember our first call, when you used to apologize for your English every two minutes. You don't do that anymore. Now you run the meeting, you negotiate the clause, you even crack a joke. The momentum you've built is real, and I don't want you to lose it. So here's my challenge for the next year: pick one big objective, something that scares you a little. For me, the obvious one is Europe. With the investment closed, you have the cash flow to expand, and your brand has the traction to do it. But the goal isn't really the market, it's the man. A year ago you would have said you couldn't lead in English. Now you can. So dream a little bigger. Let's catch up properly next week and map it out. I'm proud of you, and I'm excited for what's next. Talk soon.

Hey, this is Milton, just a voice note to myself at the end of the program. Twenty lessons. A year ago I used to write out every sentence before a call and still freeze when the phone rang. I would rehearse hello. Today I closed an investment in English, I read every clause, and I negotiated two of them without panic. That's the achievement I'm proudest of, not because of the money, but because the fear is gone. So what's next. My objective for the year is Europe, that's my big one. My aspiration, the quiet dream behind it, is to build a brand people in Paris and Milan actually know. To get there I need to sustain this momentum, keep speaking, keep pushing, and grow the team so I'm not the only one who can talk to a foreign client. The real breakthrough this year wasn't a deal. It was the day English stopped being the wall and became the tool. I have the vision now, and for the first time the ambition feels reachable. The journey isn't finished. It's finally just begun.
